name: Kapalama
desc: Historic area between Kalihi and Liliha — home to Kamehameha Schools campus, diverse working-class community, easy freeway access.
insider: Kamehameha Schools campus on Kapalama Heights is the wealthiest private school in America — the campus grounds are gorgeous during open events like Song Contest
Kapalama Canal is a neighborhood landmark — the area around it is slowly being revitalized but still has rough edges
Access to both H-1 and Likelike Highway makes this one of the most commuter-friendly spots in town — windward side in 15 min
Local plate lunch spots along King Street serve some of the cheapest meals in Honolulu — under $10 for a full meal
The Kamehameha Schools Song Contest in March is one of the most moving cultural events in Hawaii — try to get tickets
Kapalama has a strong working-class Filipino and Samoan community — neighborhood potlucks and church gatherings are common
Nimitz Highway industrial corridor runs through the lower section — expect truck traffic, noise, and warehouses
The old Kapalama fire station and post office give the area a historic small-town feel within the city
Cost of Living Market on King Street is appropriately named — one of the cheapest grocery spots in Honolulu
The neighborhood is sandwiched between busier Kalihi and quieter Liliha — it gets the overflow of both
